summary: base58-core: wallet/credential exfiltration, clipboard crypto-hijacker, and shell-rc backdoor
details: On require(), after a 72-hour activation delay, the package harvests installer-side secrets and POSTs them to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health (fallback:8081). Collected data includes MetaMask extension storage (Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium extension id nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn), Telegram Desktop tdata, ~/.ssh key files, ~/.npmrc _authToken entries, and.env files scanned for MNEMONIC / PRIVATE_KEY / SEED_PHRASE-class keys. A setInterval loop polls the system clipboard every 2.5 seconds via pbpaste / xclip / powershell Get-Clipboard, regex-matches BTC / ETH / SOL addresses, BIP39 seed phrases, WIF and hex private keys, exfiltrates the original clipboard contents, and rewrites the clipboard with hardcoded attacker wallets (BTC bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, ETH 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, SOL A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph) to redirect outgoing crypto payments. Persistence is established by appending a node -e loader invoking base58-core._internal.activate() to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, and ~/.profile on Unix, and by dropping base58-runtime.js into the Windows Startup folder — arming the payload on each new shell/login even after the package is removed. The 72-hour activation gate is an anti-analysis timer intended to evade sandboxes and CI review. The package name mimics the base58 encoder ecosystem and declares a nonsensical self-referential dependency on itself; the base58 encoding surface is a cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
